achievements.description: WC Predictor Game go-ahead confirmed by Dean Ryan — Mike created #gdc-wcpredictor channel, invited Gabriel and Miguel, then added devs Kelvin Yee, Harden Wallace and Eoin McCarthy. Project is now formally in flight.

WC Predictor legal geo sign-off received — Allowed: UK, Ireland, Germany, Austria, Denmark, Romania, Belgium, Sweden, Australia, Peru, Greece, Norway. Excluded: USA, Canada, Italy, Netherlands, Mexico, Brazil, India.

WC Predictor mechanics clarified and spec updated on Confluence (PF Spec - World Cup Predictor Game): predictions changeable until end of matchday 1 (17 June), giving late starters a window to enter and improving marketing messaging.
WC Predictor prototype updated with CMS and shared with the team at https://world-cup-predictor-alpha.vercel.app/ — this is now the source of truth for design and dev.
WC Predictor T&Cs draft sent to legal — Mike shared draft terms in group DM with Dean Ryan, Ekaterina Smolinova and John O'Shea. Ekaterina confirmed she will review Monday or Tuesday.
achievements.source: slack DM (Dean Ryan / Ekaterina Smolinova / John O'Shea)

achievements.description: Miguel Migneco confirmed to begin WC Predictor design work — Mike briefed him via DM; Miguel confirmed straightforward and will start immediately. Match settlement display approach also discussed.
Brand carousel went live on gambling.com/uk/online-casinos — Chloe confirmed in #6amigos at 11:04. Relates to GDCU-8861 shipped this week.
Yesterday's release (07/05) verified clean by QA — Robin John confirmed in #gdc-pod that all tickets that went live on Thursday passed QA checks with no issues.
GDCU-8959 (Duplicate Payment Methods on IS/BE brand reviews) created and moved to In QA same day — actioning Chloe's flag from 07/05. Assigned to Sanja Budic.
GDCU-8899 (VPN Block Research — restricting restricted-country users from FTP games) moved to In QA by Sanja Budic.

Three tickets entered In Product review queue today: GDCU-8955 (Cool Tones Phase 2, Harden), GDCU-8936 (Ace Navigation Redesign, Carlos), GDCU-8847 (VIP Rev Share Oplist, Nebojsa). All dev work complete awaiting Mike's sign-off.
HR confirmed receipt of recruitment information for 2 roles — Lauren Bradshaw replied to Mike confirming the open positions' details have been received.
OKR pages updated for H1 — 2026 H1 O1KR2 (AI use cases, ~35% complete) and O1KR3 (GDC at 95% completion, est. 31 May) updated in Confluence OKRs space.
Ciara Carroll progressed GDCU-8432 — seeking dev input on HTML vs gif file approach for a site section replacement. Gabriel confirmed the section should not be crawlable by Google.

meeting_summaries.title: WC Predictor Go-Ahead Call with Dean Ryan
meeting_summaries.time: ~Morning BST (inferred from DM thread, call confirmed)
meeting_summaries.summary: Mike had a call with Dean Ryan to discuss the World Cup Predictor Game. Dean confirmed the go-ahead. Key discussion points included prize pool (£10k mentioned, described as potentially 'biggest on site'), required design assets, and overall project structure. Mike messaged Dean: 'Right, we got the go ahead on the WC predictor game.' Following the call Mike created #gdc-wcpredictor and began mobilising the team. Prize pool amount remains TBC — Dean asked to chat further about it.
decisions: WC Predictor Game is formally approved and in flight
Prize pool ~£10k discussed but not yet confirmed — Dean wants to discuss further
action_items_from_meeting: Mike to finalise prize pool amount with Dean Ryan
Mike to confirm what design assets Dean requires
WC Predictor dev kickoff call to be scheduled (channel created same day)

meeting_summaries.title: #gdc-wcpredictor Channel Kickoff (all-day async)
meeting_summaries.summary: Mike created the #gdc-wcpredictor channel at 09:43 and progressively built out the team and spec throughout the day. Gabriel confirmed match results will be entered manually in Admin Panel; bracket dropdowns will allow team selection as tournament progresses. Legal geos shared at 14:43. Devs Kelvin Yee, Harden Wallace and Eoin McCarthy added at 12:10. Spec and prototype updated and shared. Mechanics change agreed: prediction window extended to 17 June end of matchday 1. Translations approach flagged as TBC for a separate meeting.
decisions: Match results entered manually via GDC Admin Panel — no automated feed
Admin Panel bracket: 'Seed 1A' boxes become dropdowns to select teams as tournament progresses
WC Predictor will be English-only at launch; browser translations to serve non-English territories (translations meeting pending)
Predictions changeable until end of matchday 1 (17 June) — allows late entries and improves marketing angle
Prototype at https://world-cup-predictor-alpha.vercel.app/ is source of truth for design and dev
action_items_from_meeting: Translations approach to be confirmed in a separate meeting (Gabriel's flag)
Mike to schedule KO call with dev team (Kelvin, Harden, Eoin)
Ekaterina Smolinova to review T&Cs by Monday/Tuesday

action_points.action: REVIEW GDCU-8874 — Page Bloat: Reduce HTML payload size (Harden, HIGH, In Product). Day 2 carry-over. Harden ready to proceed with 4-fix SEO plan (CookieYes lazy render saves 432KB/18,900 DOM nodes; SSR operator list saves up to 1,212KB; VWO async; inline scripts). Combined saving estimated ~58% on /uk/online-casinos. Stakeholders: Chloe, Josh, Gabriel, Dan Smullen.

REVIEW GDCU-8806 — In-App Age Gate 18+ Verification (Carlos Blauth, MEDIUM, In Product). Apple App Store Guideline 5.3 compliance gate. Carlos has built the implementation (confirms on first launch, stores preference, blocks under-18s). Approval needed before any app store submission.

REVIEW GDCU-8955 — Cool Tones Phase 2 (Harden, MEDIUM, In Product, entered queue 08/05). Jessica's UX feedback incorporated: FAQ padding, Casino Navigation container white bg + border radius, article card styling, table colour fixes, red-to-consistent link colour, comments section padding.

REVIEW GDCU-8936 — Ace Navigation Redesign with Global Nav Bar (Carlos Blauth, MEDIUM, In Product, entered queue 08/05). Shane McGrath confirmed dev work complete today. Global nav, burger menu, sign in/up UI and sidebar icon all redesigned per Figma prototype.

REVIEW GDCU-8847 — VIP Rev Share Oplist (Nebojsa Stanic, MEDIUM, In Product, entered queue 08/05). Displays custom operator list to ktag-identified VIP users (level 2+). UK only. AdTech personalisation API approach preferred.

SCHEDULE WC PREDICTOR KO CALL — Dev team (Kelvin Yee, Harden Wallace, Eoin McCarthy) in #gdc-wcpredictor. Spec and prototype confirmed. Book kickoff call for early next week. Walk devs through PF Spec, Admin Panel architecture, prediction window mechanics, and data dependencies (Karl check-in). 11 June is hard deadline for Group Bets component.

JESSICA BACK MONDAY — Review GDCU-8948 (Prize Boxes 5th Box, MEDIUM, In Product) together. Also respond to her request for feedback on 'the design she did for Colin and Dean' — identify what this refers to (likely GDCU-8906 NL Claude Tables or another piece) and provide feedback.
WC PREDICTOR — Confirm Karl check-in on WC data readiness (fixture dating and resulting data via API/matrix). Key dependency for dev build. Was outstanding all week — must be resolved before KO call.
WC PREDICTOR — Finalise prize pool with Dean Ryan. £10k discussed but Dean flagged 'biggest on site?' and wanted to chat further. Must be agreed before T&Cs are finalised by Ekaterina.

WC PREDICTOR TRANSLATIONS — Gabriel flagged 'will discuss translations in the meeting later' at 14:57 BST. Confirm outcome of that discussion: is English-only confirmed, or is a translation solution in scope? This affects build scope.

CONFIRM GDCU-8907 BETTING TIPS HUB UNBLOCKED — Sanja said 'This should be fixed now' on 07/05 for the European leagues scope issue, but ticket still showed Blocked as of morning report. Verify Jira status updated so QA can proceed.

GDCU-8810 UNBLOCK — Apple/Google Org Developer Accounts (Harden, BLOCKED, In Progress). No progress today. Identify who holds organisation credentials and escalate. This gates the entire app store submission pipeline.

GDCU-8619 OPERATOR LIST REDESIGN — Ciara now In Progress (unblocked 07/05 after 22 days blocked). Confirm first milestone/review point. Ciara also has GDCU-8432 (site section HTML/gif) and GDCU-8392 (WC Cosmo Spins reskin, Backlog, deadline 11 June) — agree sprint sequencing.

INTERVIEW COORDINATION — Liam Caffrey seeking Wednesday 12 May 12–3pm (Gabriel confirmed available). Mike's calendar looked full per morning report. Check M365 calendar and confirm or propose alternative time.

VIC DADSON — Signed off until 16 May. HR (Lauren Bradshaw) has the doctor's note. Nemanja requirements and BI knowledge sharing with Chris deferred. Brief Colin on coverage plan and ensure no critical data work is blocking sprint output.

GDCU-8878 CA/FR OPLIST REDESIGN — Moved back to In Progress today (Kelvin Yee). Major redesign of CA/FR casino pages. Note: Kelvin is also lead dev on WC Predictor — monitor capacity and flag if sequencing conflict arises.

GDCU-8846 COSMO SPINS SPRING AD CAMPAIGN — Creative direction still not provided. Josh Kerr feedback: clearer message, less 'gaming' feel. Ciara's capacity is stretched. Give direction early next week so this doesn't stall further.

DESIGN SYSTEM GUIDELINES — Team flagged Figma asset panel confusion (gradient usage, conflicting libraries: Tailwind, GDC DS, Heroicons, Material). Consider short alignment session with Miguel, Ciara and Jessica. Not urgent but a recurring pain point affecting design velocity.

INTAKE FORM — Gabriel Cornoiu's 23/04 suggestions (SEO & Information Architecture for new pages/hubs; operator offer attributes) still unconfirmed. Gabriel active on WC Predictor — ask him to confirm intake form status when next in contact.

calendar._note: Microsoft 365 authentication not completed — full calendar data unavailable. No Confluence agenda pages found for Monday 11/05/2026. Known context: (1) Ekaterina Smolinova reviewing WC Predictor T&Cs Monday or Tuesday. (2) Liam Caffrey interview targeting Wednesday 12 May 12–3pm (Gabriel available; Mike's calendar check needed). (3) Jessica Dordevic returns from leave Monday. (4) Content team sprint planning pages for May 11–17 indicate sprint kick-off week. (5) Product sync pattern is Wednesdays — no agenda created yet. (6) Friday 121 with Colin Brannigan — no Slack evidence it occurred today; if deferred, schedule early Monday.
